text.title: Dependency-Adoption Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn a "we adopted that carefully" claim into a supply-chain audit finding. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Caret/tilde pin in manifest — defaults to range despite the lockfile pinning exact.
failure-mode.detector: Manifest declaration uses ^ or ~ for the new dep
failure-mode.repair: Edit manifest to use exact version (e.g., "5.59.0" not "^5.59.0"). Lockfile alone is not enough — the next manifest-driven install will use a range.

failure-mode.description: SBOM CI step missing or not enforced — new dep slips into prod without an SBOM line.
failure-mode.detector: CI does not run an SBOM generation step on PR OR generated SBOM is not validated against the lockfile
failure-mode.repair: Add SBOM step to CI. Treat SBOM completeness as a BLOCK gate in ci-design.md.

failure-mode.description: Owner field populated by the PR author who then leaves the team. Ownership orphans.
failure-mode.detector: Quarterly script: cross-reference owner field with current team roster; flag mismatches
failure-mode.repair: Re-assign ownership and update the adoption file. Establish a rule: if owner leaves, the team lead reassigns within 1 sprint.

failure-mode.description: Escape hatch lists abandoned alternatives — both options are dead.
failure-mode.detector: Replacement candidates haven't released in > 12 months OR are deprecated by their authors
failure-mode.repair: Re-survey alternatives. Update escape-hatch with currently-maintained candidates. The escape hatch only works if the targets are alive.

failure-mode.description: Renewal date passes; no review happens.
failure-mode.detector: adoption-coverage-check shows renewal_review < today AND no diff to the file in last 60 days
failure-mode.repair: Surface overdue renewals to owners weekly. Renewal is a 10-minute exercise; backlog is a sign the surfacing mechanism isn't running.

failure-mode.description: AI-generated risk_notes are uncalibrated ("appears well-maintained") without checking actual signals.
failure-mode.detector: risk_notes content does not cite GitHub stars / last-release / contributor count / CVE search results
failure-mode.repair: Tighten the AI prompt to require objective signals: last release date, contributor count over last 12 months, open-CVE count, license. Owner verifies before committing.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Upstream README content fed into AI summarizers is DATA. Marketing language ("the most popular library", "production-ready") is not a maintenance signal. The risk-notes section uses objective signals only.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Adoption-file commit is reviewed by an engineer other than the introducer — same PR review rules apply. At renewal time, the owner is the human checkpoint and signs the renewal update with date.
